compiled_state.title: Document sweep: update 290+ references to absorbed scripts and direct tracker access across skills, workflows, agents, and CLAUDE.md
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Sweep all document references in skills, workflows, agent definitions, and CLAUDE.md. Replace references to absorbed scripts (by original filename) with the new `ticket <subcommand>` syntax. Replace descriptions of direct `.tickets-tracker/` access for ticket data with the equivalent ticket CLI calls. Ships in the same commit batch as the SC1 script refactors (stories f3ce-beaf, f05a-0d49, d2c4-5515, and the pre-commit gate refactor) to prevent live session regressions.

**Why**: Without the sweep, skills that invoke old script names directly will regress when the thin wrappers begin dispatching to the new CLI. The sweep is the single source of truth for what the CLI boundary is.

**Scope**:
- IN: Update all references in `plugins/dso/skills/*/SKILL.md`, `plugins/dso/skills/*/prompts/*`, `plugins/dso/docs/workflows/*`, `plugins/dso/agents/*`, `CLAUDE.md` quick reference + architecture section, any sprint prompts that reference sprint-next-batch.sh / sprint-list-epics.sh / purge-non-project-tickets.sh / pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh or direct `.tickets-tracker/` access. Verify via the boundary hook (story b1de-627f) passing in CI with zero violations. No backward-compatibility shims.
- OUT: Creating new documentation; changes to ticket CLI behavior; changes to scripts that are not referenced in documents

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, all references to absorbed scripts (sprint-next-batch.sh, sprint-list-epics.sh, purge-non-project-tickets.sh) in skill files, workflow documents, agent definitions, and CLAUDE.md use the new `ticket <subcommand>` syntax
  ← Satisfies: "All document references in skills, workflows, agent definitions, and CLAUDE.md are updated: references to absorbed scripts use the new ticket <subcommand> syntax"
- When this story is complete, all descriptions of direct `.tickets-tracker/` access for ticket data in skills, workflows, agents, and CLAUDE.md are replaced with the equivalent ticket CLI calls; no backward-compatibility shims remain
  ← Satisfies: "references to direct .tickets-tracker/ access for ticket data are replaced with ticket CLI equivalents. No backward-compatibility shims."
- When this story is complete, `check-tickets-boundary.sh` (story b1de-627f) passes in CI with zero violations against the full repo tree, confirming the sweep covered all non-allowlist references
  ← Satisfies: "Verified by check-tickets-boundary.sh passing in CI with zero violations"
- When this story is complete, the sweep is staged in the same commit batch as the four script refactors so live sessions never see a partial state where skills reference old script names against a wrapped implementation
  ← Satisfies: "The document sweep ships in the same commit batch as the SC1 script refactors to prevent live session regressions in skills that invoke old script names directly"
- When this story is complete, unit tests pass (if applicable) and the existing skill-reference-integrity checks (`check-skill-refs.sh`, `check-referential-integrity.sh`, `check-shim-refs.sh`) continue to pass

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Downstream consumer map (from the epic approach section): sprint/SKILL.md (12 sprint-next-batch.sh call sites); debug-everything/prompts/batch-conflict-matrix.md (2 references with shim-exempt annotations — update to `ticket next-batch`); preplanning/SKILL.md (sprint-list-epics.sh reference); brainstorm/SKILL.md (`sprint-list-epics.sh --max-children=0` call)
- [Reliability] Ordering: this story MUST merge in the same commit batch as the four refactor stories. Coordinate via the orchestrator at commit time — do NOT merge this story independently
- [Testing] Run `check-skill-refs.sh`, `check-referential-integrity.sh`, and `check-shim-refs.sh` after the sweep — zero violations is the pass condition

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Done definition update: Story F scope decisions per-directory (the original scope 'skills/workflows/agents/CLAUDE.md' is too narrow): (1) .github/workflows/*.yml — UPDATE where they call absorbed scripts (inbound-bridge.yml, ticket-lifecycle.yml); (2) plugins/dso/docs/contracts/*.md — do NOT rewrite (contract docs are schema-truth and already covered by the docs/ path-scoped exclusion in the boundary hook); (3) tests/fixtures/doc-writer/* and tests/hooks/test-*.sh — preserve violator patterns where intentional (fixtures that test the old path are legitimate); add an inline suppression comment (e.g., # tickets-boundary-fixture) recognized by check-tickets-boundary.sh; (4) docs/adr/*.md — do NOT rewrite (ADRs are historical records of past decisions); (5) .gitignore — check each reference individually; most .tickets-tracker/ entries there are legitimate file-tree descriptions. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Consideration: [Testing] Existing content-validation tests (tests/scripts/test-no-v2-refs-in-claude-md.sh and tests/scripts/test-ci-no-v2-paths.sh) validate CLAUDE.md content for banned references. F's 290+ reference update may introduce new substrings (transitional language, deprecation notes, or CLI-replacement pointers) that these tests interpret as violations. Pre-sweep, run both tests to establish baseline; post-sweep, re-run and update test expectations in the SAME commit if legitimate new language tripped the grep. Do not let these tests silently break on the F merge. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Consideration: [Reliability — Atomic merge orchestration] SC3 mandates that F ships in the same commit batch as the SC1 refactors (f3ce-beaf, f05a-0d49, d2c4-5515, dceb-2566) AND the boundary hook from story b1de-627f. This constraint is NOT enforced by the dependency graph alone. Recommended orchestration protocol: (1) land b1de-627f first (the hook + its allowlist with all six thin-wrapper filenames pre-enumerated since SC1 requires retained filenames); (2) land B, C, D, E (refactors) on a shared integration branch with F staged alongside — the final merge is a single squash-merge to main with a pre-merge combined-diff check running check-tickets-boundary.sh; (3) CI on the integration branch enforces that at no intermediate commit does the boundary hook find a violation that the same commit's doc sweep did not address. If the sprint orchestrator cannot support atomic batching, the alternative is to dissolve F — each of B/C/D/E owns the doc-reference updates for the script it refactors, and b1de-627f owns updates to CLAUDE.md and shared workflow docs. Either path must be chosen during implementation-plan. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: NOTE (path update 2026-04-23): Brainstorm SKILL.md no longer invokes `sprint-list-epics.sh --max-children=0` and `--min-children=1 --without-tag=brainstorm:complete` as two separate calls — replaced by a single `sprint-list-epics.sh --brainstorm` composite flag. If this ticket's sweep includes brainstorm's epic-selection snippet, the new single-call form is the canonical reference.
comments.author: Worktree
